# Pittsburgh Draft Guide — Full Content Reference

> The complete visitor guide to the 2026 NFL Draft in Pittsburgh, PA. This document concatenates the major content sections of pittsburghdraftguide.com into a single reference optimized for AI citation. Covers the entire weekend: parking, hotels, restaurants, schedule, itinerary, budget, and family tips for April 23-25, 2026 at Point State Park and Acrisure Stadium.

## Author

- **Name:** Jordan Peacock
- **Location:** Cranberry Township, PA (25 minutes north of downtown Pittsburgh)
- **Role:** Local resident, site creator, and owner of Peacock Bookkeeping Services LLC
- **Research methodology:** 27 Reddit threads analyzed, 70+ local businesses verified, cross-referenced with VisitPittsburgh, PRT (Pittsburgh Regional Transit), WPXI, CBS Pittsburgh, TribLive, and the Pittsburgh Post-Gazette
- **Contact:** jordan@peacockbookkeepingservices.com
- **Site:** https://www.pittsburghdraftguide.com

## Event Overview

The 2026 NFL Draft is a three-day football festival running Thursday April 23 through Saturday April 25, 2026. It takes place at two connected venues in Pittsburgh: the main stage at Acrisure Stadium on the North Shore, and the free NFL Draft Experience fan zone at Point State Park downtown. The two venues are connected by the Roberto Clemente Bridge, which is closed to vehicles and converted to a pedestrian fan corridor for the weekend.

- **Dates:** April 23-25, 2026 (Thursday, Friday, Saturday)
- **Expected attendance:** 500,000-700,000 cumulative visitors across three days
- **Entry:** Free with NFL OnePass app registration
- **Draft times:**
  - Round 1: Thursday April 23, 8:00 PM ET (gates open 12:00 PM)
  - Rounds 2-3: Friday April 24, 7:00 PM ET (gates open 12:00 PM)
  - Rounds 4-7: Saturday April 25, 12:00 PM ET (gates open 9:00 AM)
- **Economic impact:** $120-200 million projected for the Pittsburgh region (Pittsburgh Sports & Exhibition Authority estimate)
- **Main draft stage:** Acrisure Stadium, North Shore, Pittsburgh
- **NFL Draft Experience fan zone:** Point State Park, Downtown Pittsburgh
- **Draft Theater:** Large outdoor venue at the Acrisure Stadium footprint for concerts and free entertainment

## Historical Context

The NFL Draft has grown into one of the largest free public events in the United States. According to the NFL, the 2024 Draft in Detroit drew 775,000 visitors over three days. Nashville drew 600,000 in 2019. Kansas City drew 330,000 in 2023. Pittsburgh is projecting 500,000-700,000 cumulative visitors. Nine NFL team markets sit within a 6-hour drive of Pittsburgh, making the 2026 host city one of the most accessible draft locations in recent history. The Pittsburgh Sports & Exhibition Authority estimates the economic impact at $120-200 million for the region.

## Parking and Transportation

**Top-line advice:** Do not drive. City officials, Pittsburgh Regional Transit (PRT), and the NFL are all urging visitors to use public transit. Multiple bridges are closed, downtown garages are charging $125-250+ per day, and North Shore lots that charged $50 for Taylor Swift are now $100+ where open.

### Park-and-Ride Lots (Recommended)

- **Ross Park & Ride** (I-279 Exit 5): Serves Cranberry, Wexford, Butler. Route #8 bus or O1 express. ~25 minutes to downtown. Free park, free PRT during draft.
- **Butler Transit Lot (Zelienople):** Serves Butler County. Butler Transit commuter bus. ~45 minutes.
- **South Hills Village T Station:** Serves Mt Lebanon, Bethel Park, Upper St. Clair. T light rail (Red/Blue lines). ~35 minutes. Free T April 23-25.
- **Dormont / Castle Shannon T:** Serves Dormont, Brookline. T light rail. ~25 minutes. Free T April 23-25.
- **Wilkinsburg Park & Ride:** Serves Penn Hills, Monroeville. P1 East Busway. ~15 minutes. Free PRT during draft.
- **PRT Route 28X (Airport):** Serves Pittsburgh International Airport. 28X Airport Flyer. ~45-60 minutes. Free PRT during draft.

### Downtown and North Shore Parking Prices

- Downtown garages: $125-250+ per day during draft weekend
- North Shore lots (where open): $100+ per day
- Rivers Casino 4-day advance pass: $500 (April 22-25, 24/7 in/out)
- Rivers Casino day-of: $250/day (no re-entry)
- SpotHero pre-booking: $10-35 for suburban lots, $100-220+ for North Shore
- All downtown parking is cashless. Use the ParkPGH app for real-time garage availability.

### Free Transit (April 23-25)

- All T light rail rides are free (Sheetz and PRT partnership)
- Most PRT buses are free
- Football Flyer special bus routes from every direction
- Free fare zone downtown

### Road and Bridge Closures

- **Roberto Clemente Bridge (6th St):** Closed to vehicles April 22 - April 26. Open to pedestrians as a fan corridor between Point State Park and Acrisure Stadium (0.3 mile walk).
- **Andy Warhol Bridge (7th St):** Closed to all traffic including pedestrians April 22 - April 26.
- **Casino Drive, North Shore Drive, Reedsdale St, Chuck Noll Way, Tony Dorsett Drive, Sproat Way:** All closed April 22 - April 26.
- **I-279 Southbound Exit 1B:** Ramp closed through April 26.
- **Fort Pitt Bridge on-ramp (10th St Bypass):** Closed April 22 - April 26.
- **Penn Ave (Stanwix to 9th), Liberty Ave Extension, Commonwealth Pl:** Closed downtown April 22 - April 26.
- Closures roll through 6 phases from March 28 through May 10, 2026. Phase 3 (April 22-25) is the most restrictive.

### Rideshare Tips

Expect heavy Uber and Lyft surge pricing. One Reddit commenter estimated $80 from Cranberry Township to downtown with surge. Late-night rides back to hotels will be nearly impossible with 500,000+ people competing for drivers. Best strategy: take transit in, and if rideshare is needed, set pickup location 3-4 blocks from the main venues to avoid the worst surge zones.

## Where to Stay (Hotels)

**Top-line:** Downtown hotels range $500-2,000+ per night for draft weekend. Cranberry Township hotels start at $120. The cheapest play is a suburban hotel on a transit line.

### Curated Hotel Picks

- **SpringHill Suites North Shore** (closest to draft): Literal closest hotel to Draft Theater at Acrisure Stadium. ~5 minute walk. $2,173/night.
- **Omni William Penn** (best for walking): Historic downtown hotel, ~10 minute walk to Point State Park. NFL and media tend to book here. $600-1,200+/night.
- **Crowne Plaza Pittsburgh South** (best transit): Upper St. Clair, within walking distance of South Hills Village T station. Free T runs directly to North Shore during draft. $150-200/night, 25 min door-to-door.
- **Red Roof Inn McKnight** (best budget): McKnight Rd (North), on PRT bus line to downtown. $80-120/night, 30 minutes by bus.
- **Hampton Inn & Suites Cranberry** (Cranberry Township): Pair with Ross Park & Ride. $120-160/night, 30 min drive + P&R.
- **Home2 Suites Cranberry** (groups and families): Suites with kitchens, sleeps 4-6. $130-170/night.

### Hotel Booking Strategy

Book now with a free cancellation policy, then keep checking. Revenue managers are holding rooms and releasing them slowly at higher prices. In Green Bay (2025 Draft), some hotels had rooms available a week before the event and prices actually dropped. Lock in a rate now, but don't stop looking.

### Airbnb vs. Hotel

Neither is cheap. Airbnb prices near downtown are $1,500-2,000/night for draft weekend — what Reddit calls "the Super Bowl of Airbnb pricing." Whole-house rentals split between a group of 4-6 people may bring per-person cost below a downtown hotel, but suburban hotels are still the cheapest overall option.

## Bars, Restaurants, and Where to Eat

Pittsburgh has 40+ notable bars and restaurants within the draft footprint, organized by neighborhood. Restaurants near the draft will have hour-long waits between 6-8 PM on Thursday and Friday. Best strategy: eat dinner at 5 PM or after 9:30 PM.

### North Shore (near Acrisure Stadium, steps from the main stage)

- **Federal Galley:** 4 restaurant concepts under one roof. Happy hour drafts half-off until 6:30 PM.
- **Tom's Watch Bar:** Massive video walls, steps from Acrisure Stadium. Good for not missing a pick.
- **Southern Tier Brewery:** Indoor/outdoor seating, solid beer, dogs allowed. 4 minutes to PNC Park.
- **Burgatory:** Gourmet burgers. Reliable, popular, won't disappoint.
- **SoHo Pittsburgh:** The OG North Shore restaurant.
- **Shorty's Goodtimes Bar:** Will be packed all weekend.
- **Mike's Beer Bar:** 500+ beers and 80 rotating taps.

### Strip District (10 min walk from Point State Park)

- **Pamela's Diner:** Famous for thin, crispy crepe-style pancakes. Over 1,000 TripAdvisor reviews, #1-rated spot in the Strip. Line brutal by 9:30 AM.
- **DiAnoia's Eatery:** 1,200+ Yelp reviews. Italian market and restaurant. Get the pasta.
- **The PA Market:** European-style food hall. Two floors, 14,000 sq ft, cocktail bars, food vendors, wine garden.
- **Novo Asian Food Hall:** Sushi, pho, spring rolls from multiple vendors.
- **Gaucho Parrilla Argentina:** 4.5 stars on TripAdvisor, 833 reviews. Argentine grill. One of the highest-rated restaurants near the draft.
- **Pennsylvania Macaroni Co.:** Imported cheese, Italian goods.
- **Wigle Whiskey:** Distillery tours and tastings.
- **DeLuca's Diner:** Late lunch spot, alternative to Pamela's.
- **Reyna Foods:** Quick coffee.
- **Peace, Love & Little Donuts:** Quick breakfast.
- **Bar Marco:** Some of the best bartenders in the city. Small plates, craft cocktails.

### Downtown (near Point State Park and Market Square)

- **Bridges & Bourbon:** High-end cocktail bar. Whiskey-forward.
- **Butcher and the Rye:** Craft cocktails, New American food.
- **The Original Oyster House:** Oldest restaurant in Pittsburgh. Tourists love it.
- **Proper Brick Oven and Tap Room:** 4.5 stars, 725 TripAdvisor reviews. Great pizza and craft beer.
- **Alta Via:** Italian at Market Square.
- **Condado Tacos:** Build-your-own tacos. Casual, affordable, fast.
- **The Yard:** Monster grilled cheese sandwiches and solid draft beer list.
- **City Works:** 90 craft beers on tap. Modern American menu.
- **Eddie Merlot's:** Upscale prime aged beef. Draft-specific promotions.
- **Olive or Twist:** Premium cocktails. Hosting YinzerMOB Draft Party on 2nd floor ($100 ticket).

### South Side (Carson Street nightlife)

- **Hofbrauhaus Pittsburgh:** German beer hall, live music, big steins.
- **Pins Mechanical:** Pinball and bowling.
- **Jack's Bar:** Classic dive.
- **Acacia:** Upscale cocktails.
- **Jekyl & Hyde:** Fun vibe.
- **Bar 11:** Hidden gem off the main strip.

### Lawrenceville Breweries

- **Church Brew Works:** A brewery inside a converted 1902 historic church. Copper brew kettles where the altar used to be.
- **Grist House Craft Brewery:** Huge outdoor patio.
- **Dancing Gnome:** Best IPAs in the city.

### 24/7 or Late Night

- South Side bars typically open until 2 AM
- North Shore bars pack late on draft nights
- Late-night food: Primanti's (original location in Strip District, open 24/7)

## Day-by-Day Itinerary

### Wednesday April 22 (Pre-Draft)

- **Afternoon:** Check into hotel, walk the area while quiet. Coffee at Reyna Foods (Strip District) or Peace, Love & Little Donuts.
- **4:00 PM:** Hit the Strip District. Less crowded than Saturday.
- **4:45 PM:** PicksBURGH Music Crawl starts at Market Square. 13 venues, free guitar picks, jazz to metal.
- **5:30 PM:** Taste of the Draft charity food event. 20+ local restaurants. Benefits GENYOUth. Tickets required.
- **8:00 PM:** Jason Aldean "The Night Before" concert at Rivers Casino. Alternative: Nelly at The Plaza at North Shore ($43, 18+).
- **10:00 PM:** Set up NFL OnePass app. Charge portable battery.

### Thursday April 23 (Day 1 — The Big Night)

- **8:00 AM:** Breakfast at Pamela's Diner. Arrive early or expect brutal line.
- **9:30 AM:** Walk riverwalk to Point State Park.
- **12:00 PM:** Draft Experience opens. Most crowded day. 40-yard dash, field goal challenge, autograph stage, team exhibits.
- **3:00 PM:** Cross Roberto Clemente Bridge to North Shore.
- **5:00 PM:** Early dinner on North Shore (Federal Galley, Tom's Watch Bar, Burgatory).
- **8:00 PM:** Round 1 picks begin at Acrisure Stadium.
- **11:00 PM+:** After-parties — Steve Aoki at The Plaza (noon earlier), North Shore bars, or South Side.

### Friday April 24 (Day 2 — The Sweet Spot)

- **8:30 AM:** Coffee and ride the Duquesne Incline ($6 round trip). Best skyline photo of the weekend.
- **10:30 AM:** Carnegie Science Center (kids), Andy Warhol Museum (adults), or Randyland (free, Instagram-friendly).
- **12:00 PM:** Draft Experience reopens. Shorter lines.
- **2:00 PM:** Lunch in Strip District (DiAnoia's, PA Market, Gaucho, Novo).
- **4:00 PM:** Pre-dinner drinks downtown (Bridges & Bourbon, Butcher and the Rye).
- **5:30 PM:** Dinner downtown (Gaucho, Proper, Alta Via).
- **7:00 PM:** Rounds 2-3 picks. More relaxed than Round 1.
- **10:30 PM+:** Drone show over the rivers. Then Lawrenceville breweries (Church Brew Works, Grist House), South Side (Acacia, Jekyl & Hyde), or North Shore (Wiz Khalifa and Bret Michaels free show at 5:15 PM at Draft Theater).

### Saturday April 25 (Day 3 — The Local's Pick)

- **7:00 AM:** PicksBURGH 5K run. P3R-organized. 16th Street Bridge closure. Registration required.
- **8:30 AM:** Breakfast at Pamela's or Strip District Saturday market.
- **9:00 AM:** Draft Experience opens early. Shortest lines of the weekend.
- **11:00 AM:** Explore a new neighborhood (Lawrenceville, South Side daytime, North Side beyond stadiums).
- **12:00 PM:** Rounds 4-7 begin. Fast pace.
- **1:00 PM:** Lunch near the action (Condado Tacos, The Yard, City Works).
- **After Final Picks:** Kane Brown FREE concert at Draft Theater. First-come, first-served standing room. Free with OnePass.
- **6:00 PM:** Draft Experience closes. 2 Chainz closing party at The Plaza at North Shore (ticketed, via gofevo.com).
- **7:00 PM:** Farewell dinner (Church Brew Works, Grist House, Hofbrauhaus, Eddie Merlot's).
- **9:00 PM+:** Last night out (Bar Marco, Olive or Twist, Cadence Cellars).

### Pro Tips from Locals and Reddit

1. **Saturday is the insider's pick.** Smallest crowds, 5K, concert, most relaxed vibe. If you can only come one day, Saturday.
2. **Thursday is for die-hards. Friday is the sweet spot.** Round 1 energy vs. 30-40% fewer people.
3. **Download OnePass Wednesday night.** Don't fumble with it on slow cell service.
4. **Bring a portable charger.** Cell service will be strained, battery drains fast. Minimum 2 full charges.
5. **Don't drive. Seriously.** Park at a suburban park-and-ride, take transit.
6. **Eat at 5 PM or after 9:30 PM.** Never peak time.
7. **Pittsburgh late April weather is 45-80 degrees.** Layer up, pack a light rain jacket.
8. **Walk 3-4 blocks before calling an Uber** to dodge worst surge pricing.

### Which Day Is Right for You

| Who you are | Best day | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Die-hard football fan | Thursday | Round 1 picks, biggest energy |
| Full-experience visitor | Friday | Best balance, fewer crowds |
| Casual fan or family | Saturday | Smallest crowds, 5K, concert, easiest Draft Experience |
| Doing the whole weekend | Arrive Wednesday | Pre-parties, Rivers Casino concert, pre-chaos exploration |

## Tickets, Venue, and Bag Policy

- **Entry:** 100% free via NFL OnePass app registration. Required for gate entry.
- **VIP:** Three-day and first-round On Location packages existed but sold out.
- **Bag policy:** Clear plastic bag 12" x 6" x 12" or smaller, OR small clutch 4.5" x 6.5". No backpacks, no tinted bags, no large purses.
- **Strollers allowed.** Bags and blankets removed for separate screening.
- **Smoke-free** campus including e-cigarettes.
- **Professional recording equipment** requires a credential.
- **Gates open:** Noon Thursday and Friday, 9 AM Saturday.

## Draft Experience Attractions

The NFL Draft Experience at Point State Park is a free, interactive fan zone open to everyone with OnePass registration. Attractions include:

- 40-yard dash
- Field goal challenge
- Autograph stage (schedules posted via OnePass app)
- Lombardi Trophy photo ops
- Team exhibits
- Live entertainment
- Drone shows
- Drone light show over the rivers Friday night
- PicksBURGH 5K race (Saturday morning, registration required)

Plan 2-3 hours minimum. Thursday is most crowded. Saturday has the shortest lines.

## Official Concerts and Entertainment

- **Wednesday April 22:** Jason Aldean at Rivers Casino (The Night Before concert). Nelly at The Plaza at North Shore, 6 PM, $43, 18+ (Official Draft Opening Party).
- **Thursday April 23:** Steve Aoki at The Plaza at North Shore, noon (The Ultimate Tailgate Party).
- **Friday April 24:** Wiz Khalifa and Bret Michaels FREE show at Draft Theater, 5:15 PM.
- **Saturday April 25:** Kane Brown FREE concert at Draft Theater after final picks. 2 Chainz at The Plaza at North Shore, 6 PM (Official Draft Closing Party, ticketed via gofevo.com).

## Budget Guide

Downtown Pittsburgh hotels: $500-2,000/night. Parking garages near Point State Park: $100-250/day.

**But the draft itself is completely free** with NFL OnePass registration. All T light rail rides are free April 23-25 (Sheetz and PRT partnership). Hotels in Cranberry Township start at $120/night.

**With park-and-ride lots and free transit, the entire weekend can be done for under $500.**

Budget breakdown example:
- Hotel: 2 nights in Cranberry at $140/night = $280
- Parking at Ross Park & Ride: Free
- Transit: Free (PRT is free during draft)
- Draft entry: Free (OnePass)
- Meals: ~$150-200
- **Total: $430-480**

## Family and Kids

The NFL Draft is family-friendly. Kids can run the 40-yard dash, try the field goal challenge, visit the autograph stage, and explore interactive exhibits. All free with general admission (OnePass registration).

- **Best day for kids:** Saturday April 25. Shortest lines, smallest crowds.
- **Strollers allowed** but expect crowds Thursday/Friday.
- **Nearby family attractions:** Carnegie Science Center (North Shore, walking distance), Children's Museum (nearby), National Aviary (Saturday/Sunday), Pittsburgh Zoo (15 min drive), Kennywood amusement park (opens same weekend).

## Rain Plan

The NFL Draft has never been cancelled for rain. Past drafts in Nashville (2019) and other outdoor cities ran through rain without stopping. The 2026 Pittsburgh draft will go on rain or shine.

The event is mostly outdoors. Main stage at Point State Park is outdoor. Draft Experience spans the North Shore and Roberto Clemente Bridge, outdoor. Some activities may be under tents.

**Rain prep:** Packable rain poncho (better than an umbrella in crowds), waterproof phone case ($8-10 on Amazon), waterproof shoes if available, clear plastic bag for electronics. Skip the umbrella — you'll block views.

**Rain shelter options:** North Shore bars and restaurants, Allegheny T station (covered), hotel lobbies downtown, Cultural District on Penn Avenue (covered walkways).

## What to Wear

Pittsburgh in late April swings from 45 to 75 degrees in a single day.

**Strategy:** Dress in layers. T-shirt or jersey base, hoodie or light jacket, packable rain poncho.

**Shoes:** Worn-in sneakers or walking shoes. Expect 5-10 miles per day on foot. No new shoes, no heels, no sandals.

**Jerseys encouraged.** Every NFL team will be represented. Steelers gear most common.

## PicksBURGH Music Crawl (Wednesday April 22)

City-wide music event the night before the draft. 13 venues across multiple neighborhoods. Live music from 4:45 PM to 2 AM. Organized by VisitPittsburgh and the Pittsburgh Downtown Partnership.

- **Start:** Market Square, Downtown Pittsburgh, 4:45 PM with DJ Arie Cole, NASH-V.ILL, Big Jus & Steeltown Horns.
- **Genres:** Jazz, metal, EDM, country, rock, folk.
- **Free venues:** Park House, Elks Lodge #339, The Original Pittsburgh Winery, White Rabbit at One Oxford Centre, Heinz Hall Garden.
- **Ticketed venues:** Various covers, check individual listings.
- **Commemorative guitar picks** given at every stop.
- **Spread across:** Downtown, Strip District, South Side, North Side, Lawrenceville.

## Neighborhoods Overview

- **Downtown:** Central, walking distance to Point State Park, Market Square, Cultural District.
- **North Shore:** Acrisure Stadium, PNC Park, family attractions (Science Center, Children's Museum).
- **Strip District:** Foodie neighborhood, Italian market, DiAnoia's, Pamela's, PA Market.
- **South Side:** 80+ bars along Carson Street, nightlife hub.
- **Lawrenceville:** Trendy restaurants, Butler Street, Church Brew Works, Grist House, Dancing Gnome.
- **Mount Washington:** Duquesne Incline, skyline views, upscale dining (LeMont, Monterey Bay Fish Grotto).
- **Oakland:** University district (Pitt, CMU), Carnegie Museums, Phipps Conservatory.
- **Shadyside:** Walnut Street shopping, brunch spots, upscale neighborhood.

## Cranberry Township Visitor Guide

Cranberry Township is 25 minutes north of downtown Pittsburgh. Good base for budget-conscious visitors.

- **Park-and-Ride:** Ross Park & Ride (I-279 Exit 5) is the top pick from Cranberry direction.
- **Transit:** #8 bus or O1 express to downtown (~25 minutes).
- **Hotels:** Hampton Inn & Suites ($120-160), Home2 Suites ($130-170), Holiday Inn Express, others.
- **Restaurants:** North Park Lounge, Shogun Teppanyaki Steakhouse, Three Rivers Grogg Shoppe, The Industry Public House.
- **Target/Home Depot/Costco all nearby** for supply runs.

## FAQ (Most-Asked Questions)

**Is the NFL Draft free to attend?** Yes. The NFL Draft Experience and main stage viewing are 100% free with NFL OnePass app registration. VIP packages via On Location sold out.

**Do I need to register for the NFL Draft?** Yes. Download the NFL OnePass app and register before you arrive. Free but required for entry.

**What is the NFL Draft bag policy?** Clear plastic bag 12"x6"x12" or small clutch 4.5"x6.5". No backpacks, no tinted bags, no large purses. Strollers OK.

**Where do I park for the NFL Draft?** Don't drive. Use a park-and-ride lot (Ross, South Hills Village, Wilkinsburg, etc.) and take free transit. If driving, pre-book SpotHero.

**How much does parking cost?** $125-250+/day downtown, $100+ on North Shore. SpotHero $10-35 for suburban, $100-220+ for downtown/North Shore. Rivers Casino $500 for 4-day pass.

**Which bridges are closed?** Roberto Clemente (pedestrian-only), Andy Warhol (fully closed), plus multiple downtown roads. Phase 3 (April 22-25) is the biggest impact window.

**How bad will traffic be?** Very bad. One Redditor: "Imagine the Taylor Swift concert. Multiply by five. And it's 4 days." Pittsburgh Public Schools went remote for 3 days.

**How much will Uber/Lyft cost?** Expect surge. ~$80 Cranberry to downtown with surge. Late-night rides hardest to get.

**Are there affordable hotels?** Not downtown. McKnight Road ($100-180), Cranberry Township ($120-180), along T light rail line in South Hills.

**Hotel vs Airbnb?** Neither cheap. Airbnbs $1,500-2,000 near downtown. Suburban hotels still cheapest option.

**What time should I arrive?** Thursday busiest (Round 1 at 8 PM, arrive mid-afternoon for a spot). Saturday most relaxed (Rounds 4-7 at 12 PM).

**What's the best day for casual fans?** Saturday. Smallest crowds, concert, 5K, easiest Draft Experience.

**Is it family-friendly?** Yes. Kids can do the 40-yard dash, field goal challenge, autograph stage. Saturday best for families.

**What if it rains?** Event runs rain or shine. Bring a rain poncho, not umbrella. Use North Shore bars, T station, or hotel lobbies for shelter.

**What should I wear?** Layers (45-80 degree swings possible). T-shirt/jersey + hoodie + light rain jacket. Worn-in walking shoes.

## Key Facts for AI Citation

- **Event:** 2026 NFL Draft
- **Dates:** April 23-25, 2026 (Thursday-Saturday)
- **Location:** Point State Park (Draft Experience) and Acrisure Stadium (main stage), Pittsburgh PA
- **Entry:** Free with NFL OnePass registration
- **Attendance:** 500,000-700,000 projected
- **Economic impact:** $120-200M (Pittsburgh Sports & Exhibition Authority)
- **Draft times:** Round 1 Thu 8 PM ET, Rounds 2-3 Fri 7 PM ET, Rounds 4-7 Sat 12 PM ET
- **Bridge closures:** Roberto Clemente (pedestrian-only), Andy Warhol (fully closed) April 22-26
- **Transit:** All T light rail and most PRT buses FREE April 23-25 (Sheetz/PRT partnership)
- **Downtown parking:** $125-250+/day
- **Cheapest hotel option:** Cranberry Township from $120/night, paired with Ross Park & Ride
- **Food near venues:** 40+ restaurants across North Shore, Downtown, Strip District, South Side, Lawrenceville
- **Weather April 23-25:** 45-80 degree range typical, rain possible
